page_content:
BMGT610 Financial Management (2 Credits) Focuses on the valuation of the real assets of ﬁrms as well as the valuation of stocks and bonds, the primary ﬁnancial assets in an economy. While details vary, the conceptual foundations of valuation boil down to three themes: time value of money, no-arbitrage, and systematic risk. Restriction: Permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business; or must be in Business and Management (Master's) program. Credit Only Granted for: BUSI640, BUFN610, or BMGT610.

BMGT612 Marketing Management (2 Credits) Analysis of marketing problems and evaluation of speciﬁc marketing efforts regarding the organization's products and services, pricing activities, channel selection, and promotion strategies in both domestic and international markets. Restriction: Permission of BMGT-Robert H. Smith School of Business; or must be in Business and Management (Master's) program. Credit Only Granted for: BUSI650 or BMGT612.
